user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: scripts/thefly_summarize.py
## Active selection of the file:
gpt-4o-mini
## My request for Codex:
write me `scripts\models.py`
a base class to represent AI models
and classes that act as that base class
the base class presents to the caller a way to transcribe any audio file specified by file path, and writes a JSON file in the specified path
the base class presents to the caller a way to summarize any transcription json file, and writes a markdown file in the specified path
models don't have to implement both things, just throw a not implemented exception
the scripts `scripts\thefly_summarize.py` and `scripts\thefly_transcription.py` will be modified to use this
there will be a factory function that takes model names and returns the correct actual model class

each model class is responsible for all the requests, which is all done through OpenAI's API, BUT, most of the models are actually using Ollama with the server pretending to be the OpenAI API but using the local IP
For actual OpenAI models, assume the API key is in the environment already
For transcription, have support for `gpt-4o-transcribe` and `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` `Whisper` and `Faster-Whisper` and `Parakeet`
For summarization, have support for `gpt-4o-mini`, Qwen 3, Gemma 3, Llama 3.3, DeepSeek-R1
agent_reply: - `AIModel` base class for transcription to JSON and summarization to Markdown.
- Local OpenAI-compatible/Ollama-style models: Whisper, Faster-Whisper, Parakeet, Qwen 3, Gemma 3, Llama 3.3, DeepSeek-R1.
- `create_model(...)` factory, including tagged local ids like `qwen3:8b` and `llama3.3:70b`.

Refactored [scripts/thefly_transcription.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_transcription.py>) and [scripts/thefly_summarize.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_summarize.py>) to use the model layer. I also kept the old script-level functions/constants as thin compatibility wrappers so [scripts/thefly_desktop.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py>) still imports cleanly.
python -m py_compile scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py
No live API requests were made. There are still unrelated pre-existing dirty files in the worktree; I left those alone.
- `AIModel` base class for transcription to JSON and summarization to Markdown.
- Local OpenAI-compatible/Ollama-style models: Whisper, Faster-Whisper, Parakeet, Qwen 3, Gemma 3, Llama 3.3, DeepSeek-R1.
- `create_model(...)` factory, including tagged local ids like `qwen3:8b` and `llama3.3:70b`.

Refactored [scripts/thefly_transcription.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_transcription.py>) and [scripts/thefly_summarize.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_summarize.py>) to use the model layer. I also kept the old script-level functions/constants as thin compatibility wrappers so [scripts/thefly_desktop.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_desktop.py>) still imports cleanly.
python -m py_compile scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py
No live API requests were made. There are still unrelated pre-existing dirty files in the worktree; I left those alone.

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: scripts/thefly_desktop.py
## My request for Codex:
`scripts\thefly_transcription.py` got a `Prompt is not supported for diarization models`, so please do not include a prompt if the model is the diaization model
agent_reply: `OpenAICompatibleTranscriptionModel` now has `supports_prompt = True`, and `GPT4oTranscribeDiarizeModel` sets `supports_prompt = False`. That means `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` skips both explicit `--prompt` and the automatic filename-derived prompt.
python -m py_compile scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py

`OpenAICompatibleTranscriptionModel` now has `supports_prompt = True`, and `GPT4oTranscribeDiarizeModel` sets `supports_prompt = False`. That means `gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize` skips both explicit `--prompt` and the automatic filename-derived prompt.
python -m py_compile scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: scripts/thefly_audio_decryptor.py
## Active selection of the file:
E
## My request for Codex:
implement `scripts\audio_file_shortener.py`
use argparse, take in a path to an audio file
also allow the user to specify an output file path, if the output file path is not specified, use the input file's directory.
also let the user choose a silence level, but this is optional, use a sensible default
open the file, decode it into a PCM stream, stereo if the file is stereo
analyze the file into a list of silence chunks, each chunk represented by a start and stop time, and can provide a duration
divid the file into big sections, if the file is under 22 minutes, leave it alone, otherwise, find the best way to split the file so that each section is between 12 minutes and 18 minutes.
now we know where the rough split points are, go look around the first split point, within a search window of 2 minutes, find the largest silence period.
The middle of the largest silence is where we split. take the audio before the split, go through it again ensuring all silence periods are 3 seconds or shorter.
save it as "<output file path without extension>.part1.<wav>"
then repeat, saving each processed large section as "<output file path without extension>.part<n>.<wav>"
if a section is shorter than 22 minutes, don't bother dividing it, but still remove silence from it.
This function will either return the original file path if the original file is under 22 minutes and thus need no splitting, or, it returns a list of new file paths.
`scripts\thefly_transcription.py` needs to import this new file and new function, and use it before submitting to the AI model, as the models are rejecting large files.
agent_reply: - Decodes audio to 16-bit PCM WAV, preserving channel count, using WAV directly when possible and `ffmpeg` otherwise.
- Detects silence chunks as `SilenceChunk(start, stop)` with `.duration`.
- Leaves audio alone if it is <= 22 minutes and returns the original path.
- For longer audio, splits into roughly 12-18 minute sections, choosing the midpoint of the largest silence within 2 minutes of each rough split.
- Caps long silence inside each part to 3 seconds.

Transcription now calls `shorten_audio_file()` before submitting audio. If the file is split, it transcribes each part and combines the results into one JSON with combined `text`, adjusted `segments`, and a `parts` record.
Also updated [thefly_summarize.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_summarize.py:107>) so summarizing an audio file uses the same transcription wrapper.
python -m py_compile scripts\audio_file_shortener.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py scripts\models.py
I also ran synthetic WAV tests: short audio returned the original path; a 23-minute test file split into two WAV parts and reduced the long silence around the split. No live AI/API calls were made.
- Decodes audio to 16-bit PCM WAV, preserving channel count, using WAV directly when possible and `ffmpeg` otherwise.
- Detects silence chunks as `SilenceChunk(start, stop)` with `.duration`.
- Leaves audio alone if it is <= 22 minutes and returns the original path.
- For longer audio, splits into roughly 12-18 minute sections, choosing the midpoint of the largest silence within 2 minutes of each rough split.
- Caps long silence inside each part to 3 seconds.

Transcription now calls `shorten_audio_file()` before submitting audio. If the file is split, it transcribes each part and combines the results into one JSON with combined `text`, adjusted `segments`, and a `parts` record.
Also updated [thefly_summarize.py](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/scripts/thefly_summarize.py:107>) so summarizing an audio file uses the same transcription wrapper.
python -m py_compile scripts\audio_file_shortener.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py scripts\models.py
I also ran synthetic WAV tests: short audio returned the original path; a 23-minute test file split into two WAV parts and reduced the long silence around the split. No live AI/API calls were made.

user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: scripts/thefly_summarize.py
## My request for Codex:
the script is running, it is painfully slow and there's no output in the terminal, I have submitted an hour long real meeting...
provide print outputs for the splitting, and how long each split is
then as they are being submitted into the models, have the base class print out which files are being uploaded and also print out when the model returns a result
agent_reply: You’ll need to stop/restart the currently running script for these changes to take effect.
New split/shortener output goes to the terminal immediately, for example:
audio shortener: decoded 1:02:13 audio, 2 channel(s), 16000 Hz
audio shortener: analyzing silence at -42 dBFS
audio shortener: writing part 1/4 from 0:00 to 15:42 (15:42 before silence trimming)
audio shortener: wrote meeting.part1.wav (14:55 after silence trimming)
Model calls now print from the shared model layer:
GPT-4o Transcribe Diarize [gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize]: uploading meeting.part1.wav, 54.2 MB for transcription
GPT-4o Transcribe Diarize [gpt-4o-transcribe-diarize]: returned transcription result for meeting.part1.wav
GPT-4o mini [gpt-4o-mini]: submitting meeting.trans.json for summarization
GPT-4o mini [gpt-4o-mini]: returned summarization result for meeting.trans.json
python -m py_compile scripts\audio_file_shortener.py scripts\models.py scripts\thefly_transcription.py scripts\thefly_summarize.py scripts\thefly_desktop.py
I also ran mocked model-call smoke tests and a generated long-WAV split test; no live API/model calls were made.

"openai-whisper;small" or "openai-whisper;large-v3"
and these might need to use a subprocess call
or
"faster-whisper;small;cpu;int8" or "faster-whisper;large-v3;cuda;float16"

these will use `from faster_whisper import WhisperModel` and `model = WhisperModel("small", device="cpu", compute_type="int8")` or some code like that
agent_reply: I changed `whisper` / `openai-whisper` and `faster-whisper` to local pip-backed transcription models:
I also kept `whisper-1` as the actual OpenAI API Whisper model, so that path is still available separately.

I also ran factory parsing checks and fake-module smoke tests for both local Whisper paths. Note: `scripts/models.py` is still untracked in git from the earlier work, so remember to add it when you commit.
